rulings.Case Title: NORTHERN CALIFORNIA DISTRICT COUNCIL OF LABORERS VS. MERCER FRASER COMPANY A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, June 13, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER NORTHERN CALIFORNIA DISTRICT COUNCIL OF LABORERS 'Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioner Northern California District Council of Laborers' petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the petition was not opposed and a proposed judgment which conforms to the relief stated in the arbitration award and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CARISSA COPELAND VS. MOGOS BERHE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Consolidate Claims
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, June 13, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CARISSA COPELAND'S Notice Of Motion To Consolidate Claims. Plaintiff Carissa Copeland's motion to consolidate this case with two uninsured motorist arbitrations is denied. While consolidation of court litigation with an uninsured motorist arbitration is permissible, Ms. Copeland failed to provide admissible evidence that this case has a significant relationship with the two uninsured motorist arbitrations to warrant denial of the insurers' contractual right to have the uninsured motorist claims determined by arbitration. The only evidence submitted by Ms. Copeland regarding the relationship between this case and the two uninsured motorist arbitrations is her counsel's belief based on the hearsay statement of an unnamed "expert consultant" that "all the injuries Plaintiff suffered stemmed from the 2014 and 2015 Collisions." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEEN PHONE LEE ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Intention To Move For A New Trial
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, June 13, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS GEEN LEE, GEEN LEE, JOHNNY LEE, CALVIN LEE, AND GEEN LEE'S Notice Of Intention To Move For A New Trial. Plaintiffs' motion for new trial is denied. Plaintiffs fail to show that the February 20, 2018 order granting summary judgment in favor of defendant City and County of San Francisco was erroneous. After another full review of the moving, opposition and reply papers on the summary judgment motion as well as review of the papers filed on this motion, the court remains of the view that the undisputed facts establish that the physical characteristics of the intersection do not constitute a dangerous condition of public property, whether considered separately or in conjunction with the blind spots from the vehicle driven by Mr. Dilinia. The new authorities cited by plaintiffs, their elaboration of their prior arguments and their contentions that the February 20, 2018 order contained errors of fact and law do not persuade the court that there is a triable dispute whether the intersection constituted a dangerous condition of public property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY K LEE VS. HOSIE RICE LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint Under Anti-Slapp Statute (Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16)
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Wednesday, June 13, 2018, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT ANTHONY LEE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint Under Anti-Slapp Statute (Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16). Cross-defendant Anthony Lee's anti-SLAPP motion to strike both causes of action alleged in the cross-complaint filed by Hosie Rice LLP, Spencer Hosie, and Diane Rice is granted as to both causes of action. Mr. Lee satisfied his first prong burden of showing that both causes of action arise from petitioning activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Mr. Lee commenced his action to recover fees on January 3, 2017. Paragraphs 43-44 of the cross-complaint allege that Mr. Lee's e-mail to Hosie Rice on December 29, 2017 and his threat to file a motion for preliminary injunction is the conduct that alleged improperly locked up Hosie Rice's XRP options. Communications during pending litigation implicate petitioning activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. (Digerati Holdings LLC v. Young Money Entertainment (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 873.) Cross-complainants have not satisfied their second prong burden of showing that there is minimal merit to either of their claim. The litigation privilege codified in Civil Code 47 bars liability based on the conduct alleged in paragraphs 43-44 of the cross-complaint. Cross-complainants argue that their claims are also based on communications that Mr. Lee had with Ripple that resulted in the locking the options. This argument fails because the court is obligated to take the cross-complaint "as it is." Cross-complainants cannot "amend" the cross-complaint pleading to defeat this motion. (Premier Medical Management Systems, Inc. v. California Ins. Guarantee Assoc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 464, 476.) "The question is what is pled-not what is proven." (Comstock v. Aber (2012) 212 Cal.App.4th 931, 942.) "Thus, the rule in section 425.16, subdivision (b)(2) that a court must consider affidavits as well as pleadings in the first step of the anti-SLAPP procedure does not provide license to ignore the allegations of a plaintiff's complaint¿We therefore conclude that, if the complaint itself alleges acts included within section 425.16, subdivision (e), there is no reason to go beyond the scope of those allegations to determine whether a plaintiff's claims arise from protected conduct." (Bel Air Internet, LLC v. Morales (2018) 20 Cal.App.4th 924, 937.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Lee is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BENNETT GOLDBERG ET AL VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Stephens Institutes Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena To Elisa Stephens And, In The Alternative, For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 13, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE's Motion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena To Elisa Stephens And, In The Alternative, For A Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required unless the parties inform the court by 4pm that the matter has been resolved. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: KWAN WO IRONWORKS INC. VS. THOMPSON BUILDERS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Kwan Wo Ironwork Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 13, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF KWAN WO IRONWORKS INC.'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion if granted in part and denied in part. Because Defendant served mostly complete verified responses before the hearing date, the Motion to Deem Requests for Admission Admitted is denied. Defendant must serve complete responses to form interrogatories 9.1(c), 12.1, 15.1 and 17.1 within 15 days, and if it has not already done so, serve documents responsive to Request for Production within 15 days. Late service of discovery responses does not relieve Defendant of sanctions. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $2,155. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)
